Study of Acts

Week by Week

The Study

The whole book, a passage at a time — Scripture to read, questions worth sitting with, and a blank page for whatever Sunday stirs up.

Go Deeper

Insights

Longer reflections drawn from the study — the background, the turns in the argument, and the through-lines worth slowing down for.

Quiet Time

Devotion

Seven readings for the week, with room to answer — unhurried time to let the passage do its work on you.

The Story Out Loud

Listen

Teaching and conversation for the drive, the walk, or the quiet part of the morning — the message brought close enough to hear.

Acts  ·  Study Journal
Before you begin

How to Use
This Study Guide

The Acts Study Guide is laid out in weekly lessons with resources for daily engagement to help us grow together.

Each lesson includes
Alongside the lessons

Additionally, there are several articles throughout the guide that provide important context to aid you in your study.

The appendices include helpful maps that link geography with key first-century events that shaped the early church. You’ll also find a valuable resource designed to equip followers of Jesus in sharing their faith.

Use this guide wherever you study the Book of Acts—in your personal time with God, in small group meetings, and in corporate worship. Discover how the formation and experiences of the early church can guide and encourage you today.

Need help?

This study guide and included bookmark are designed to help you understand and apply the Scriptures accurately to your life. The Inductive Bible Study Method is an effective tool in accomplishing that goal. This method equips you to make key observations in a passage of scripture, interpret it within its proper context, and then apply it to daily life.

For a brief introduction to the Inductive Bible Study Method, The Training Center of Fellowship Bible Church has prepared helpful videos to get you started.

play_circle Watch the intro videos fellowshipnwa.org/acts open_in_new

Additionally, The Training Center offers Personal Bible Study, a class that goes much deeper into this method of study. If you have not yet taken this class, we encourage you to do so to experience the riches that come from intentional exploration of God’s written Word.

event Sign up for Personal Bible Study trainingcenternwa.org/events open_in_new mail Contact the Training Center training@fellowshipnwa.org open_in_new
Discussion guide suggestions

This guide helps small groups engage in discussion. Use discretion in determining which questions will work best and be most beneficial. You will likely find a few questions for each lesson that will prompt your group into deeper discussions. You can even create questions of your own.

The prompts begin with broad “getting to know one another” questions and lead into deeper observations of the passage, application thoughts, and prayer suggestions.

Blessings to you and your group as you journey together through the Book of Acts.
